context: Regular class — discovering repertoire barre, advanced barre: pliés, jetés, fondus, frappes, adage, grand battement, allegro 1–3

corrections.text: Discovering repertoire barre: clearer heads on croix
Discovering repertoire barre: listen to the music, don't rush
Relevé in fifth: leaning away from the barre and wobbly on demi-pointe
Relevé in fifth: back not engaged, shoulder came up
Batterie preparation: glutes and rotators not engaged — took tucking the pelvis to find neutral
Arches more lifted but overall weight still in heels
Tendus, glissés, grand battement at barre: more floor pressure, articulate through demi-pointe, use all the music
Plié: arms and legs must arrive together
Plié: more resistance at the bottom
Grand plié: lower heels earlier
Cambré back: engage glutes and hamstrings
Jetés: more push off the floor and hold at 45 — stronger accent throughout
Jetés: right elbow drops in second — engage lats and back to keep it placed
Fondus: gripping the barre during rond en l'air in second on demi-pointe (self-corrected)
Fondus: more resistance in the fondu extends en croix
Fondus: back leg more behind and more pulled up (self-corrected — improved)
Fondus: hand on barre more light outside of rond en l'air (self-corrected — improved)
Frappés en croix: accent out needs to be stronger
Frappé exercise: pattern still not fully clear
Frappé exercise: point second foot in the opening glissade
Adage: chassé en arrière — keep back heel down
Adage: posé arabesque en avant — keep shoulders down and square
Adage: grand rond de jambe — keep working leg at even height from side to back
Adage: lift up standing side — don't let it collapse or lean onto the barre
Adage: attitude — thigh higher, angle greater than 90 degrees
Adage: rotate working side ribcage forward
Grand battement: highest demi-pointe on retiré
Grand battement devant: weight forward, don't sit back in the hip
Grand battement derrière: back leg more square
Grand battement to second: less lifting of working hip
Grand battement: more floor pressure and articulate through demi-pointe
Allegro 1: heels down
Allegro 2: working on batterie — entrechat trois devant and derrière; need to build stamina when cleared to jump
Allegro 3: greater sense of going up — use the plié to push upwards
Allegro 3: point second foot in glissades
Allegro 3: arms before legs — use arms to gain momentum
